---
title: "Lily Peck"
image: images/Sweetie2.jpg
toc: false
about: 
  template: jolla
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 17em
  links:
    - icon: linkedin 
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/yourlinkedinname/
      target: _blank
    - icon: github 
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/test
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:ldpeck@ucla.edu
    - icon: bluesky
      text: bluesky
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/lilypeck.bsky.social
      target: _blank
---

I’m an evolutionary biologist with broad interests in the ecology and adaptation of natural populations of plants and fungi. I use whole-genome sequence data along with population genomics and comparative phylogenetics to better understand how these processes contribute to evolutionary change. I'm currently a postdoctoral scholar at the University of California Los Angeles, Ecology and Evolutionary Biology Dept. working with Prof. Victoria Sork. Specifically, I am currently working on genetic and epigenetic responses of tree populations to climate change. I received my PhD from Imperial College London in the Barraclough lab, where I used cryogenically preserved living isolates to track fungal evolution over a timescale of decades. Click through the tabs for more information about the projects and publications which I am involved with.
